title: Photoluminescent radiative cooling for aesthetic and urban comfort
methods: spectro-fluorescence-photometry (SFP) — novel two-step PL decoupling methodology
outdoor field cooling tests with feedback-controlled heater (Changchun and Hong Kong)
Mie/Rayleigh scattering engineering via SiO2 nanoparticle size selection
EnergyPlus v22.1.0 building energy simulation (19 ASHRAE climate zones)
Princeton Urban Canopy Model (PUCM) for urban canyon microclimate
UTCI (Universal Thermal Climate Index) for thermal comfort quantification
DGP (Daylight Glare Probability) for visual comfort quantification
UV ageing (ASTM-154), accelerated weathering (GB/T 9755-2014), natural outdoor exposure
data_sources: Laboratory-fabricated PLACs (PDMS + rare-earth-doped phosphors + SiO2 nanoparticles)
Outdoor field weather stations (Changchun, Oct 2024; Hong Kong, Jan 2025)
EnergyPlus v22.1.0 with PNNL DOE mid-rise apartment prototype
PerkinElmer Lambda 1050+ UV-VIS-NIR spectrophotometer with integrating sphere

data_scope.data_availability: Source data XLSX provided with article; model code on request

core_conclusion: Photoluminescence-based aesthetic composites (PLACs) using rare-earth-doped phosphors in a PDMS matrix achieve vivid coloured radiative cooling with effective spectral reflectance exceeding 100% (peak 141%) while maintaining 90.2-93.2% overall solar reflectance. In outdoor tests PLACs kept surfaces 2.2-3.9 °C below ambient and up to 7.9 °C below non-PL counterparts. Critically, moderate whiteness reduces sunlight trapping in urban canyons, cutting mean radiant temperature by up to 9.3 °C, thermal discomfort periods by 8.6%, and visual discomfort periods by 5.8% — resolving the colour-cooling trade-off and the urban microclimate penalty of super-white PRC surfaces simultaneously.
why_it_matters: Resolves the fundamental colour-cooling dilemma by using photon down-conversion rather than scattering-based whiteness; introduces the SFP measurement standard that corrects systematic spectrophotometer errors for PL materials; quantifies the underrecognized urban microclimate penalty of super-white coatings at global scale; projects 13-63 GJ per year cooling energy savings across climate zones; demonstrates scalable substrate compatibility and durable field performance.
why_nature: Direct decarbonisation pathway for building air conditioning at global scale; cross-disciplinary fusion of photonics, materials science and urban climate modelling; global 19-city evaluation converting a laboratory result into transferable policy input; bioinspired design (coral photoadaptation) with engineering rigour; advances SDG 11 (sustainable cities) and SDG 13 (climate action).
added_value_to_my_research: Satellite-derived urban canyon albedo maps + PLAC MRT benefit curves -> spatially prioritized PLAC deployment layers using remote-sensing and KDE methods
Physics-constrained ML surrogate for PUCM -> test bed for physics-informed graph learning applied to urban thermal modelling
UTCI/MRT thermal comfort deficits overlaid with socioeconomic vulnerability -> thermal equity metric extending my 2SFCA spatial accessibility framework
Neighbourhood-level PLAC-induced peak-demand savings -> headroom maps for my EV charging efficiency-equity optimization
SFP PL decoupling insight -> correction algorithm for satellite urban albedo products, improving fidelity of my multi-source data fusion pipelines

figures.caption: Design principle and visual appearance of PLACs — coral-inspired schematic, phosphor emission spectra, SiO2 nanoparticle scattering efficiencies, and room-light vs UV vs thermal-imaging comparison of PLACs and NPACs.

Optical and chromatic optimizations — SFP method schematic, solar reflectance vs PL contribution at varying pigment volume fractions, CIE 1931 chromaticity, and effective spectral reflectance exceeding 100% at emission peaks.

Cooling performance and energy savings — outdoor field test temperatures in Changchun (3 Oct 2024), net cooling power measurements (~28-44 W m-2), and annual cooling energy savings across 19 ASHRAE climate zones.

Urban thermal and glare evaluations — MRT distribution over Hong Kong, MRT vs albedo in canyons, global 19-city TDP reduction map, and false-colour daylight intensity images showing glare reduction by PLAC.

Functionalities and durability — multi-substrate compatibility, large-scale sample, superhydrophobicity (155° contact angle), waterproof textile demonstration, and solar reflectance stability after UV/natural/accelerated ageing.
